Title: Loss-of-Function Mutation in CER2-LIKE1 Reduced Accumulation of Cuticular Wax and Susceptibility to Thrips in Welsh Onion

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: WT; naturally occurring wax-deficient mutant gl
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Allium fistulosum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: forms a hydrophobic barrier and contributes to biotic/abiotic stress protection; associated with insect interaction
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation step supplying wax components above C28
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: C28 very-long-chain fatty acid elongation intermediates in conjunction with KCS11
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C30 very-long-chain fatty acids that supply wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: functions with AfKCS11 in the fatty acid elongase complex to enable elongation of VLCFAs from C28 to C30
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream to downstream VLCFA elongation step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor pool for cuticular wax components above C28/C30
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of cuticular wax components above C28, especially C30-derived wax constituents; loss of function causes an 82% reduction in total wax load and reduced ketones such as 16-hentriacontanone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AfCER2-LIKE1 is the causal gene underlying the glossy wax-deficient gl phenotype in Welsh onion. 2. A coding-sequence 1-bp deletion causes a frameshift and premature truncation, while a promoter LTR retrotransposon insertion strongly reduces transcription. 3. AfCER2-LIKE1 localizes to the ER and interacts with AfKCS11. 4. Yeast co-expression showed AfCER2-LIKE1WT, but not AfCER2-LIKE1gl, enables elongation of VLCFAs from C28 to C30. 5. Overexpression in Arabidopsis and transient expression in gl leaves increased wax accumulation/crystal deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-segregation genotyping in F2 by KASP; gene cloning and sequence analysis; qRT-PCR; promoter LUC/GUS assays; subcellular localization; MbY2H; LCI; BiFC; yeast heterologous co-expression with GC-MS fatty acid profiling; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient expression in Welsh onion; wax GC-MS; SEM
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for VLCFA elongation from C28 to C30, but not stated as the rate-limiting enzyme itself
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in leaves; markedly reduced to nearly undetectable in gl; tissue pattern examined across successive leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic Arabidopsis, overexpression increased transcript abundance and stem wax content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field and laboratory thrips infestation assays; heterologous expression in yeast; overexpression in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduced wax accumulation but increased resistance to thrips; no obvious growth difference between WT and gl
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering Welsh onion wax composition and insect-response traits; loss-of-function allele/gl mutant may be valuable germplasm for thrips-resistant breeding, whereas functional allele may be used to restore or enhance wax accumulation depending on deployment goals
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression/transient expression; native AfCER2-LIKE1 WT and gl promoters for reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic sequencing of WT and gl plants reported previously; no public accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of function is caused by both promoter retrotransposon insertion at -439 bp with 11-bp TSD and a coding-region single-base deletion; mutant protein fails to interact with AfKCS11

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: forms protective cuticular barrier and affects plant-insect interaction
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / cuticular wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: VLCFA elongation before and at the C28-to-C30 extension step
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase (KCS) family; condensing enzyme of the FAE complex
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: very-long-chain acyl-CoA elongation substrates up to C28
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: elongated VLCFA intermediates; with AfCER2-LIKE1 supports C30 VLCFA production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the condensing step in VLCFA elongation and, together with AfCER2-LIKE1, supports extension from C28 to C30
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream VLCFA elongation; elongase complex condensing step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies/directly generates precursor pool for wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports VLCFA elongation and C30 fatty acid synthesis when co-expressed with AfCER2-LIKE1; alone increases C28 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AfKCS11 is an ER-localized KCS6 ortholog in Welsh onion. 2. It physically interacts with AfCER2-LIKE1WT but not the mutant AfCER2-LIKE1gl. 3. In yeast, AfKCS11 alone elevated C28 fatty acids, and co-expression with AfCER2-LIKE1WT significantly promoted C30 fatty acid synthesis. 4. The study indicates AfKCS11 acts in the elongase complex underlying wax precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: subcellular localization; MbY2H; LCI; BiFC; yeast heterologous expression with GC-MS fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: described in the paper as the rate-limiting enzyme in the fatty acid elongase complex
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate partner gene for stacking with AfCER2-LIKE1 to modulate VLCFA-derived wax accumulation in breeding or metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive PMA1 promoter in yeast heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes that AfKCS11 function itself is not impaired in gl; the defect lies in failure of mutant AfCER2-LIKE1 to form the functional complex
